creator: volker@vwsoft.com
text:
Charles,

thanks for the information. Checking the sources and CVS, your device
should be supported since RELENG_6 (and up).

I'm wondering if you can also post your /etc/X11/xorg.conf?

By checking the archives it seems there's been a DRI problem (fixed
sometime around 6.1-R) so I would assume you'll get working DRI by updating.

Please be warned: If you're going to source upgrade your system (cvsup
sources, cvsup ports, build kernel/world, portupgrade) you'll run into a
compiler war by doing an X.org 7.x upgrade. You may probably want to do
a binary upgrade if you want to upgrade at all.

But first I would like to check your current installation. At least the
PCI-ID of your device (0x2572) is known by DRI and supported by i915.ko

You may also want to try `Xorg -configure` to have X.Org create a new
xorg.conf (save your current xorg.conf before trying that) and see if
that does it's job better.

Also you should try to fix your monitor settings (see (WW)arnings in
your xorg.0.log). Also the V_BIOS checksum error messages in xorg.0.log
makes me wondering about your device (but that may not be the source of
your trouble).

Volker
